items.summary: The former mayor and councillor of Tasmanian's second-largest city resigns effective immediately, years after an ABC investigation uncovered historical allegations about his conduct towards young men in the theatre scene.

items.title: SA government has 'assurances' from LIV Golf as Adelaide course upgrade under fresh scrutiny
items.summary: The South Australian government has reaffirmed its commitment to a $45 million upgrade of the North Adelaide public golf course to host LIV Golf in 2028, and has "assurances" about the tour's future.

items.title: Man not criminally responsible for bashing and choking elderly grandmother in Sydney hospital
items.summary: A man charged with choking and bashing an elderly woman at a Sydney hospital who later died has been found not criminally responsible due to a psychotic episode and likely schizophrenia.

items.title: Sunshine Coast massage therapist faces more sexual assault charges
items.summary: A Queensland massage therapist arrested in February over the alleged sexual assault of four clients is facing 11 fresh charges after another four female clients came forward.

items.title: Geelong fire could cause 'short-term petrol price shock' for Victoria
items.summary: As Australians woke to the news of a major fire at the Geelong refinery — one of the nation's two remaining oil refineries — questions quickly turned to what this could mean for petrol prices.

items.title: AFL Tribunal 'firestorm' taking heavy toll on players, says Saints coach Ross Lyon
items.summary: St Kilda coach Ross Lyon says the damage from a "firestorm" around the AFL Tribunal is weighing heavily as his club signals it may appeal against Lance Collard's suspension.

items.title: NRL six-again rule should be scrapped, according to coach Ricky Stuart
items.summary: Canberra Raiders coach Ricky Stuart says the NRL has lost the fabric of its game, leading to anger over the league's controversial six-again rule change.

items.title: Unemployment rate remained at 4.3pc in March, in 'calm before the storm'
items.summary: Australia's unemployment rate remained at 4.3 per cent in March, but economists say cracks could start appearing in coming months.

items.title: Two Melbourne men found guilty of manslaughter over 1999 death of Canberra woman Irma Palasics
items.summary: Two Melbourne men are found guilty of the manslaughter of Canberra woman Irma Palasics during a violent home invasion in 1999.

items.title: Son charged with mother's murder in Far North Queensland
items.summary: The woman's body was found at an address in Mareeba in Far North Queensland.

items.title: Tasmanian government defers debate on greyhound racing ban bill to avoid possible defeat
items.summary: The Tasmanian government is deferring debate on its bill to ban greyhound racing, in the hopes of eventually winning over enough upper house members for it to pass.

items.title: Investigation launched into Papua New Guinea Defence Force recruitment processes following soldier protest
items.summary: A small group of officers deserted their posts this week in protest over alleged government corruption.

items.title: LIV Golf CEO says 2026 season will not be interrupted despite rumours Saudi Arabia PIF may cut funding
items.summary: LIV Golf will run as planned in 2026 according to the league's CEO, as media reports suggest its funding from Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund may soon end.

items.title: Mother fears children may have been filmed with hidden cameras in Horsham Town Hall change rooms
items.summary: A mother says she is disturbed after learning of hidden cameras found in the theatre change rooms her children used in November with a lack of dates adding to parents' concerns.

items.title: Court releases video showing scuttling of Maria Louise K
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Household spending up in March as fuel costs surge, over-65s lead spending growth, CBA data shows
items.summary: Australians spent more in March as the outbreak of the Iran war drove up petrol prices. Older Australians increased their spending at the fastest pace, CBA data shows.

items.title: Refinery hit by blaze says fuel production secure, gas leak under investigation — as it happened
items.summary: Energy Minister Chris Bowen says the facility is still producing diesel and jet fuel but "at reduced levels, as a precaution". Follow our coverage as it happened live.

items.title: Refinery blaze may impact Australia's petrol production 'for some time'
items.summary: Energy Minister Chris Bowen says the fire in Geelong appears to be an accident at this stage and there are no suspicious circumstances.

items.title: Josh Hazlewood helps Royal Challengers Bengaluru to IPL win over Lucknow
items.summary: Josh Hazlewood has produced another masterful display in the IPL to rocket Royal Challengers Bengaluru back to the top of the league.

items.title: Global tour operator Intrepid invests millions in Central Australia's iconic Ooraminna Homestead
items.summary: Australian-based global tour operator Intrepid has announced a multi-million-dollar investment in Central Australia's Ooraminna Homestead, 30 kilometres south of Alice Springs.

items.title: WA Police stands by moderation strategy despite racist comments on social media posts
items.summary: Racist comments are being published on WA Police social media posts with little moderation, but the force says it is not prepared to switch off comments.

items.title: Four-year-old boy dies in hospital after being hit by vehicle on Dubbo street
items.summary: A four-year-old boy has died in hospital after being hit by a car on a suburban street in Dubbo.

items.title: Alex de Minaur loses to Hamad Medjedovic at Barcelona Open
items.summary: Alex de Minaur's uneven season has hit another road bump as Australia's number one succumbed to a shock last-16 loss at the Barcelona Open.

items.title: Woman claims 'toxic dose' of wrong drug during routine surgery at PRH left her brain damaged
items.summary: A 36-year-old woman who says she suffered a "hypoxic brain injury" when she was given a "toxic dose" of the wrong drug during routine surgery at Palmerston Regional Hospital is suing the Northern Territory government for negligence.

items.title: With diagnosis rates steadily increasing, men need to talk about prostate cancer
items.summary: Michael Platts panicked when he was diagnosed with prostate cancer. Now he wants to help others break through the stigma and silence surrounding the condition.

items.title: Albury Wodonga Health review identifies series of errors before man's death by suicide
items.summary: Blake Whittington, 23, was taken to hospital after a suicide attempt. He was discharged, then referred to a support service that did not contact him until days after he took his own life.

items.title: Coroner considers if partner contributed to man's death after life support was switched off
items.summary: The Coroner's Court of Victoria is investigating whether the partner of a dying Royal Melbourne Hospital patient contributed to his death in 2021.

items.title: SA government apologises to prisoner denied abortion medication
items.summary: The South Australian ombudsman calls for changes to medical monitoring at the Adelaide Women's Prison after a female prisoner was denied a medical abortion, which the health minister says is unacceptable.

items.title: Coalition's proposal to block non-citizens from first homebuyers 5 per cent deposit scheme cops backlash
items.summary: Multicultural groups are concerned Angus Taylor's proposal to prevent permanent residents from accessing the Australian Government 5% Deposit Scheme could deter skilled migrants from coming to Australia.

items.title: South-west Qld councils pitch community insurance mutual after premiums rise by 500 per cent
items.summary: Adam Osborne was quoted a staggering $60,000 for his home insurance this year. With others in the same boat, six Queensland councils are looking at creating their own insurance mutual to drive down premiums.

items.title: Victoria's short-stay levy may be contributing to drop in holiday rental growth, data suggests
items.summary: Data suggests the growth in short-stay accommodation listings in Victoria has slowed in the year after the Victorian government's new tax came into effect.

items.title: Albanese government's frustration rises alongside fuel prices and inflation
items.summary: Fuel prices and inflation aren't the only metrics climbing as the Strait of Hormuz remains shut. The Albanese government's level of frustration is rising just as fast.

items.title: These fuel supply dashboards claim to know what's really going on. Can you trust them?
items.summary: An ABC NEWS Verify investigation raises questions about popular independent fuel security dashboards that have emerged online, including one whose creators are linked to the Liberal Party.

items.title: Russia continues aerial assault as Ukraine spruiks drone advantage to allies
items.summary: Western officials and analysts say Ukrainian forces have disrupted Russia's planned spring offensive and managed to retake territory.

items.title: Israel takes out key bridge in Lebanon, Hegseth threatens Iranian infrastructure
items.summary: An Israeli strike has severed the last bridge linking southern Lebanon to the rest of the country ahead of mooted talks between Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Lebanese President Joseph Aoun.

items.title: Uncertainty over fuel supplies after major fire at oil refinery in Geelong
items.summary: An "unprecedented" fire has broken out at one of two remaining oil refineries producing fuel for Australians, with residents in parts of Geelong told to stay indoors due to smoke.

items.title: Defence spending to lift by $53 billion over the next decade as government warns of 'intensifying' risks
items.summary: Australia's defence spending will climb by $53 billion over the next decade amid what the federal government says are "intensifying" global risks.

items.title: Wednesday finance with Alan Kohler
items.summary: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

items.title: Taylor's migration plan doesn't make much sense. That's not the point
items.summary: More than a day after Angus Taylor unveiled the first plank of the Coalition's long-awaited migration policy and many media interviews later, it's still unclear what the opposition is actually proposing to change.

items.title: Anthony Albanese meets with Sultan of Brunei to shore up fertiliser and fuel supplies
items.summary: Brunei may be able to help Australia weather the economic chaos caused by the Iran war by sending additional supplies of fertiliser, the prime minister says.

items.title: Power confirms it will appeal after AFL Tribunal releases reasons for Zak Butters guilty finding
items.summary: The AFL Tribunal releases its reasons for finding Zak Butters guilty of abusive and insulting language to umpire Nick Foot, saying it was "satisfied to the requisite standard" that he had made the remarks and that it was "implausible" that the umpire would have made it up.

items.title: Man cleared of stalking Israeli President Isaac Herzog, pleads guilty to harassing Jewish school security guard
items.summary: Rajab Ali was arrested in February while President Isaac Herzog was in Sydney to pay tribute to the 15 people killed in the Bondi Beach terror attack.

items.title: Victoria Police investigating claim Katy Perry sexually assaulted Ruby Rose
items.summary: Melbourne-born actress Ruby Rose claims US pop star Katy Perry assaulted her at a nightclub in 2010.

items.title: PNG hotel owner looks to sue Australian government for $11m in unpaid refugee accommodation costs
items.summary: A hotel in Papua New Guinea is attempting to sue the Australian government over accommodation costs for refugees previously detained on Manus Island.

items.title: Petrol drive-offs prompt arrest after alleged theft of $12,000 worth of fuel
items.summary: A 36-year-old man has been charged with theft amid an investigation into as many as 20 petrol drive-offs, which police say involved an SUV that contained a tank used to carry stolen diesel.

items.title: New images released with Hobart stadium's Macquarie Point precinct master plan
items.summary: A master plan for the precinct surrounding the future AFL stadium in Hobart has been unveiled, while the ABC understands the company behind New Zealand's premier stadium in Christchurch is among those competing to build it.

items.title: Tropical cyclone Sinlaku causes significant damage in Northern Mariana Islands and Guam
items.summary: A super typhoon with ferocious winds and heavy rains made landfall in the Commonwealth of Northern Mariana Islands (CNMI) overnight, destroying homes and infrastructure.

items.title: Wright Prospecting awarded royalties from Hope Downs mine after legal battle with Gina Rinehart
items.summary: Gina Rinehart is dealt a blow, with a court finding the family of her father's former business partner has the right to potentially hundreds of millions of dollars in royalties from lucrative Pilbara mining assets.

items.title: Austin Ballard committed to stand trial over alleged fatal kick during late-night brawl
items.summary: The man accused of delivering the blow that later killed a 22-year-old on the Gold Coast has been committed to stand trial.

items.title: Man, 26, found dead following violent disturbance in Milingimbi at meeting about unrest
items.summary: A man has died after a violent disturbance in Milingimbi yesterday, in which police allege some people were armed with edged weapons.

items.title: How white should your teeth actually be?
items.summary: Experts say there are many factors that influence how white a tooth appears, and it's not just about whitening or what we’re eating.

items.title: King Charles to woo Trump and restore 'closest of friendships' on US visit
items.summary: King Charles will have tea and a private meeting with US President Donald Trump during his US state visit later this month, with trips to Washington, New York and Virginia.

items.title: Super Rugby Pacific's Moana Pasifika say 2026 season will be team's last due to finances and other challenges
items.summary: Citing financial, operational, and strategic barriers that have made continuing unviable, battling Super Rugby Pacific outfit Moana Pasifika reveal they will disband at season's end.

items.title: Zak Butters verbal abuse verdict raises questions about AFL's 'conflict of interest' with gambling
items.summary: The reporting of Port Adelaide player Zak Butters for umpire abuse sparks debate about whether officials should be involved with betting and if better audio recordings should be available from games.

items.title: Lance Collard's hearing and monster sanction leaves no winners, as queer community further bruised
items.summary: Lance Collard is handed a monster suspension after being found guilty of using a homophobic slur in the first AFL disciplinary hearing.

items.title: NSW pharmacists will soon be able to prescribe the contraceptive pill
items.summary: Patients will no longer have to visit the doctor to be dispensed the contraceptive pill, under a new pharmacy prescription scheme.

items.title: Outback nurse Hamish Briggs captures life working in remote WA on social media
items.summary: Amid chronic nursing shortages in Western Australia's regions, one nurse highlights the profession's unique benefits and captures his experience on social media.

items.title: Lebanon, Israel agree to direct negotiations after Washington talks
items.summary: Israel and Lebanon agree to direct negotiations following talks in Washington hailed by US Secretary of State Marco Rubio as a "historic opportunity" for peace.

items.title: Family hopes epilepsy Senate inquiry will shine light on funding gaps
items.summary: Four-year-old Leo has a rare form of epilepsy, and his family has turned to creative ways to fund help for him. An advocacy group says others are in the same boat, and it hopes a new inquiry will help.

items.title: Tributes flow for 'extraordinary' Robe sculptor and horsewoman Patricia Moseley
items.summary: Across dusty outback tracks to the promenades of Adelaide, sculptor Pat Moseley turned limestone and bronze into the faces of legends.

items.title: Heavyweight boxer Oleksandr Usyk puts WBC title on line against kickboxer Rico Verhoeven
items.summary: World heavyweight boxing champion Oleksandr Usyk says it is time to do what he wants ahead of his title bout against a kickboxer who has only had one boxing fight.

items.title: Coroner calls for more e-scooter safety awareness after 14-year-old's death in country Victoria
items.summary: A coroner calls for increased public safety campaigns after a 14-year-old boy died after colliding with a ute while riding an e-scooter in regional Victoria.

items.title: Australia's Cameron Green' poor IPL season continues in Kolkata Knight Riders' loss to Chennai Super Kings
items.summary: Cameron Green has suffered another challenging outing in the Indian Premier League as he struggles to live up to his tag as the most expensive overseas player.

items.title: Gosford Hospital leading Australia with new lifesaving stroke prevention surgery from US
items.summary: A NSW hospital becomes the first in Australia to offer a new stroke-prevention treatment that has been available in the United States for a decade.

items.title: The outback jobs disappearing as tourists can no longer afford fuel to travel
items.summary: From roadhouses to stations, outback workers are losing their jobs as tourists stay away. Skyrocketing fuel prices, as well as concerns around the availability of fuel in remote locations, are causing a 76 per cent loss of revenue for tourist-dependent businesses.
